headline: Warsh's debut FOMC delivers HAWKISH SURPRISE: 9 of 18 dots project a 2026 HIKE (median 3.8% vs 3.4% in March); statement cut 62%; Warsh declined to submit own dot — SPX -1.21% / NDX -1.34% on the print
summary: The FOMC voted 12-0 to hold the funds rate at 3.50-3.75% Wednesday, but the dot plot delivered a thesis-altering hawkish shift in the Summary of Economic Projections. Median 2026 year-end dot moved to 3.8% from 3.4%, eliminating cuts and pricing one 25 bp HIKE before December — six members project two hikes. The 2026 PCE forecast was raised to 3.6% from 2.7% three months ago. Chair Kevin Warsh declined to submit a personal dot, shortened the statement by 62%, announced five task forces (communications, balance sheet, etc.) and signaled openness to fewer than 8 meetings per year. Market reaction: SPX -1.21%, NDX -1.34%, RUT -0.7%; SOX held resilient (ARM +5.69%, AMAT +4.35%) as semis traded on Iran-relief and Broadcom-extended bull case. Read-through for tracked AI infra: a HIKE-tilted dot plot pressures the long-duration nuclear / utility cohort (CEG, VST, NEE) and tightens hyperscaler financing comps for ORCL ($20B ATM equity + $20B debt plan) and CRWV (European HY roadshow in flight). Confirms our note from yesterday — the 55% BoA-survey hawkish call was on the money. Watch August CPI for any Iran energy-disinflation offset that can reverse the duration impulse.

headline: Trump confirms Apple-Intel US chip manufacturing deal via Truth Social — INTC +9% pre-market on largest foundry customer validation yet; TSMC concentration risk crystallizes for Apple
summary: President Trump announced via Truth Social early Thursday that Apple has agreed to work with Intel to design and manufacture chips domestically. Intel shares jumped 9% in pre-market trade; Apple is up only ~0.6%. Neither company has formally confirmed. Structurally: this is the THIRD external 18A foundry validation in two weeks after Google's 3M TPU order and NVDA's Feynman platform evaluation, and follows the federal government's 9.9% equity stake in Intel from 2025. The bear case is that the deal is initial-scope only (Apple's lower-end M-series for select iPad / Mac, NOT the flagship M5/M6 — which TSMC retains via N2 / A16). The bull case is sequencing: any real Apple production at 18A is the most credible signal yet that Intel's foundry pivot is commercially viable, and dovetails with Musk's TerraFab + NVDA pipeline. Read-through: (1) clean positive INTC — the company is now in the 'price for the option' multiple-rerate phase; (2) modest negative TSM — concentration premium starts compressing if even ONE flagship customer diversifies away; (3) re-validates BofA's $135 double-upgrade thesis on Intel from May. House Rating: monitor — if either company confirms formally today, an INTC thesis update is warranted.

headline: Jabil (JBL) Q3 FY26 BEAT and RAISE: $3.16 adj EPS vs $3.10 est, $8.75B rev vs $8.64B (+12% YoY); FY26 AI revenue outlook lifted to $13.6B from $13.1B; FY26 total rev guide raised to $35B from $34B — clean read for ANET/SMCI/VRT cohort
summary: Jabil reported fiscal Q3 2026 results before the open Thursday with a clean beat-and-raise. Adj EPS $3.16 vs $3.10 est ($0.05 beat); revenue $8.75B vs $8.64B est (+12% YoY). The marquee data point is the FY26 AI-related revenue outlook lifted to $13.6B from $13.1B — a ~$500M raise this far into the fiscal year that confirms hyperscaler AI-server demand is pacing AHEAD of the supply chain's planning case. FY26 total revenue guide lifted to $35B from $34B. Intelligent Infrastructure segment commentary positive on the network-switching, optical, liquid-cooling, and AI-server CM volumes. Read-through: (1) cleanest positive read for SMCI (shared hyperscaler-AI-server channel concentration) and VRT (cooling content per rack); (2) supports ANET / AVGO networking-pull-through into Q3 CY26; (3) directly counters Macquarie's June 4 Google-TPU-insourcing margin-risk thesis for AVGO. The $13.6B AI rev number is the H2 setup for the entire contract-manufacturing channel — Foxconn / Quanta competitive read incrementally important. Watchlist candidate for an add discussion.

headline: US-Iran 14-point MoU virtually signed by Trump and Pezeshkian: PERMANENT cease, Hormuz reopening, $300B reconstruction, full US sanctions removal, frozen-asset release — clears the geopolitical risk premium for AI capex duration
summary: Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian virtually signed a 14-point Memorandum of Understanding Wednesday formalizing the Iran ceasefire. Terms: (1) immediate, permanent cessation of military operations; (2) US naval blockade removed, Iran facilitates Hormuz commercial transit free for an initial 60-day period; (3) phased US sanctions lift including Treasury waivers for Iranian oil exports and release of frozen Iranian assets; (4) US-backed reconstruction program for Iran worth at least $300B; (5) Iran reaffirms it will not pursue nuclear weapons, both sides will negotiate the enriched-stockpile mechanism over a 60-day window. Trump signed in Paris during a Macron meeting; the MoU took effect immediately. Oil reaction: Brent below $68, WTI ~$65, full ~17% drawdown from conflict peak now held with structural overhang. Read-through to AI infra: (1) energy-disinflation impulse should bleed into August CPI — the cleanest path to a Fed dovish re-pivot from the freshly hawkish dot plot; (2) the nuclear/utility long-duration cohort (CEG, VST, NEE) gets a cross-current — lower oil = lower fortress-energy bid offset by clearer rates path; (3) for hyperscaler capex paper (ORCL, CRWV), cheaper financing optics improve at the margin even with hawkish dots.

headline: HIVE Digital BUZZ HPC closes $220M sovereign-AI GPU contract with Bell Canada / Cohere — 2,304 Grace Blackwell GPUs at Bell AI Fabric Merritt BC; +10% pre-market; ~$70M ARR; sovereign-AI pattern repeats with non-hyperscaler underwrite
summary: HIVE Digital Technologies announced a $220M, three-year GPU cloud contract with Bell Canada and AI firm Cohere via wholly-owned subsidiary BUZZ HPC. Scope: deployment of 2,304 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell GPUs at Bell's AI Fabric facility in Merritt, British Columbia, to serve as the dedicated compute layer for Cohere's enterprise foundation models for Canadian government and corporate clients. All infrastructure remains in Canada (sovereign-AI structure). Adds ~$70M in annual recurring revenue and lifts HIVE's contracted HPC revenue book above $100M. Stock +10-11% pre-market on the print. Read-through: (1) third major sovereign-AI deal of 2026 (after Korea SK Telecom GW-scale cloud, Reliance India 168 MW Meta) — the structural pattern is non-hyperscaler national-scale capacity contracted multi-year off NVDA Grace Blackwell silicon; (2) net positive for NVDA's Blackwell book and validates the small-cap neocloud thesis (CRWV, IREN, Sharon AI peer set); (3) cleaner read on Cohere TAM — first time the company's compute footprint has been disclosed at this scale. HIVE is not in House coverage but the deal is a Cohere / Bell / NVDA data point.

headline: KOSPI closes above 9,000 for the FIRST TIME ever (+2.25% to 9,063.84); SK Hynix +6.51% to record KRW 2.685M, Samsung +4.62% to KRW 362.5k — Iran MoU + Cook memory-shortage WSJ remarks override hawkish FOMC; 100 advancers vs 810 decliners (concentration extreme)
summary: Korea's KOSPI broke 9,000 for the first time in history Thursday, closing +2.25% at 9,063.84 on a chip-led rally that defied the hawkish Fed dot plot. SK Hynix +6.51% to a record KRW 2.685M; Samsung Electronics +4.62% to KRW 362.5k — both new ATHs. Drivers: (1) Iran 14-point MoU formalization removed the Hormuz tail-risk that had been overhanging Korean export sentiment; (2) Tim Cook's WSJ interview lamented the memory-chip shortage caused by surging AI demand and signaled product-price hikes plus semiconductor investment support — read as a structural demand bid for Korea HBM4 / HBM4E supply; (3) Apple-Intel headline did NOT trigger a Korea-chip selloff because the consensus is Intel takes only the low-end M-series, leaving Samsung's foundry-pivot thesis intact. Concentration warning: Daishin Securities flagged only ~100 stocks advanced vs ~810 declined — the KOSPI move is being entirely carried by Samsung + SK Hynix + a handful of HBM-chain names. Read-through: incrementally positive for MU heading into the June 24 print (HBM ASP confirmation), positive for the SOCAMM2 supply chain (005930.KS / 000660.KS / MU), confirms the HBM4E supply / 'shortage to 2030' narrative re-engaged after CRWV's BNP Paribas initiation.

headline: Accenture (ACN) Q3 FY26 BMO: full-year revenue growth guide narrowed to 3-4% local currency (ex-US-federal 4-5%); AI consulting bookings remain the marquee read for whether AI-services is structural or rehearsal-for-automation
summary: Accenture reported fiscal Q3 2026 results before the open Thursday. Headline: full-year FY26 revenue growth guide narrowed to 3-4% local currency, or 4-5% excluding an estimated 1% federal-business drag from DOGE-driven contract review. Sell-side framing pre-print: AI-services bookings are now the most-watched line item across enterprise IT, with the June 18 print described as 'the most specific public evidence yet of whether AI engagement work converts into real revenue.' Implications for tracked names: (1) Microsoft / Oracle hyperscaler partner channel — ACN is a top-3 Azure and OCI implementation partner; weak AI-services growth would be a yellow flag for hyperscaler enterprise penetration; (2) Palantir crosscheck — high ACN AI bookings reduce the bear case that 'enterprise AI = just AWS-direct'; (3) IBM / PLTR / SNOW competitive triangulation. The federal drag is still the largest swing factor — DOGE contract reviews remain the active overhang for the entire federal IT cohort. Not currently in House coverage but the read-through is sector-defining for enterprise AI thesis.

headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -3% pre-market to ~$186 after FOMC drawdown reversed Tuesday's $225.64 ATH; Anysphere/Cursor $60B all-stock deal momentum digests; perp pricing implies further compression as duration trade unwinds
summary: SpaceX (SPCX) — six trading days post-IPO — traded down 3% pre-market to ~$186 Thursday, extending the FOMC-driven slide from Tuesday's record intraday $225.64. The stock fell intraday 5.6% Wednesday on the hawkish dot plot. Context: SPCX's $60B all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (parent of Cursor) was announced Monday June 16; Cursor's annualized revenue is ~$2.6-4B depending on the source. The deal closes Q3 pending regulatory clearance and adds frontier-AI coding tooling to the SpaceX equity story. Stock dynamics: (1) underwriter overallotment was fully exercised June 15 ($85.7B total raise — largest IPO in history); (2) the stock is now in price-discovery mode within a wide $135-$226 range and rate-sensitivity is structural; (3) Cursor deal arithmetic creates a 'paying with currency' dynamic where SpaceX trades on a 1.7T+ valuation premium that compresses with any duration repricing. Read-through to House coverage: the SPCX-Cursor deal sets a benchmark M&A multiple for AI-software peers (PLTR is closest comparison in the public list); the duration sensitivity confirms long-duration AI risk is the most exposed sleeve to the new Warsh-era dot plot.

headline: Nuclear/utility duration squeeze: CEG and VST face cross-current as hawkish dots tighten discount rate even as Iran MoU + Hormuz reopening lowers fortress-energy risk premium — sector pause signal into Q2 earnings setup
summary: The combination of Wednesday's hawkish FOMC dot plot (median 3.8% by year-end 2026) and Thursday's confirmed US-Iran 14-point MoU creates a directional cross-current for the AI-nuclear cohort. (1) Hawkish dots compress long-duration EV/EBITDA multiples for CEG (Constellation), VST (Vistra), and NEE (NextEra) — all three are intrinsically rate-sensitive given 20-year hyperscaler PPA economics priced off long-end discount curves. (2) Iran ceasefire formalization + Hormuz reopening removes the fortress-energy bid that had supported the cohort through May-June. (3) Counter-positive: yesterday's FERC approval of 760 MW capacity-rights transfer to Crane Clean Energy Center (CEG's TMI restart for the Microsoft Azure PPA) materially de-risks project economics. Net: directionally neutral-to-negative for the next 4-6 weeks unless August CPI confirms energy-driven disinflation and re-pivots dot expectations. Sympathy moves to watch: BWXT, TLN, SMR (Long-duration SMR cohort vulnerable as rates back up — was the same call we flagged June 5). House Rating: CEG, VST, NEE remain Buy on structural thesis but tactical setup softer post-print.
